Heading: tracker
Description: Unique identifier for point/user combo

Heading: longitude
Description: Longitude

Heading: latitude
Description: Latitude

Heading: stratumIdIntPts
Description: Stratum the point belongs too. (See stratification methods here.)

Heading: reEval
Description: Point was revisited (1)/ Point was not revisited (0)

Heading: user
Description: Interpreter ID number.

Heading: Across Epochs
Description: Most recent interpreter observation.
Average of all interpreter observations.
Epoch # (1-n represent epochs; 9999 respresents unpaired, not revisited, overflow points)

Heading: Value Type
Description: Interpreter probability full value 0-100
Interpreter probability converted to binary (1 is >=50, 0 is <50)

Heading: Time Period
Description: December 2021 to January 2023
December 2021 to July 2022
December 2021 to May 2022 (April was too cloudy to be used for interpretation.)

Heading: Change Type
Description: Probability that natural vegetation increased during the time period.
Probability that natural vegetation decreased during the time period.
Probability that natural vegetation did not change during the time period.

Heading: Image_Condition
Description: Magnitude of the effect that image quality had on their probability values (helped/hindered/NAN: 0-100). For example, if clouds or haze covered the point of interest then this would lower their probability/certainty.

Heading: Spatial_Context
Description: Magnitude of the effect that spatial context had on their probability values (helped/hindered/NAN: 0-100). For example, if the point was on the edge of a change/not change then the answer may be different if the image was misregistered at all, so their probability/certainty was lowered.

Heading: Spectral_Capacity
Description: Magnitude of the effect that their belief that the sensor has/does not have the radiometric capability to detect the change seen had on their probability values (helped/hindered/NAN: 0-100). For example, if it appears that maybe only a small amount of thinning occurred, their probability/certainty would be lower sincethe signal of the change may or may not be overshadowed by noise. (Robert explains this better, I need to ask him again.)

Heading: id
Description: Unique identifier for point/user combo

Heading: plotId
Description: Unique identifier for point

Heading: Valid_Name
Description: Original user name

Heading: Comment
Description: User's comments on how they made their decision.

Heading: User_IP
Description: User's IP address when they made the observation.

Heading: user_elapsed_time
Description: Time it took for user to decide their probabilities, contibuting factors to uncertainty, and comments.

Heading: Valid_Date
Description: Date that observation was made.
